items.reason: DEC-001 through DEC-020 in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` each carry id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and projects_to with concrete artifact references. The required new platform-shape decisions (DEC-016 dictionary direction, DEC-017 canonical fields, DEC-018 enum crosswalks, DEC-019 closed privacy classes, DEC-020 migration generation, plus DEC-011/012/013/014/015) are all present and align with `docs/decisions/decisions-needed.md`.

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` lists PEND-001 (owner Andy, target 2026-05-15, blocker product priority) and PEND-005 (owner Andy, target 2026-05-03, blocker CI capacity), each with rationale. No decisions are silently TBD, and decisions-needed.md cross-references the resolved set.

Each decision's consequences names concrete artifacts removed: DEC-001 'Removes per-function subject-to-person merge branches from `supabase/functions/lti-launch-handler/index.ts`...'; DEC-016 'Removes direct edits to `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json`...'; DEC-019 'Removes `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` handling from `scripts/generate_caliper_core.py`...'. Multi-surface preservation in DEC-014 is justified against the simpler 'delete all static mirrors' alternative.

Each decision lists three credible options bracketing the design space — e.g. DEC-016 contrasts 'commit only shared dictionary and generate on demand' (more aggressive), 'keep per-spec JSON canonical' (more permissive), and the chosen middle. DEC-005 considers AGS-as-canonical, peer source-native stores, and the chosen line_item/result split — all real production patterns.

`scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` reads `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` and writes the five `dictionary/*.v1.json` projections; VERIFY.md runs `--check` and `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` re-invokes it to fail on drift. The seed's projection_summary shows 59 objects and 574 fields — not a stub.

`data/data-dictionary.seed.json` defines `canonical.identity.person.email` with projections to `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.email`, `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor.email`, `dictionary/qti-core.v1.json#qti_candidate.email`, and `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership.email`. The same convergence pattern is applied to display_name, source_subject_id, and identity_kind, satisfying DEC-017 at the data layer.

`canonical.identity.person` exists as a first-class entity in `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` with fields (id, display_name, email, source_subject_id, identity_kind), allowed values, and an explicit projections array mapping OneRoster `person`, Caliper `caliper_actor`, QTI `qti_candidate`, and LTI `lti_membration`/`lti_launch.subject_id`. Unification is data, not just DEC-001 prose.

Each per-spec dictionary's enum field carries a `shared_enum_id` (e.g. `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json` shared_enum 'action' with both crosswalk and reverse_crosswalk). `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py.check_shared_enum` verifies bidirectional mappings exist for every enum value. The grading_progress enum cross-walks both OneRoster `fullyGraded` and LTI `FullyGraded` to one canonical value.

DEC-019 enumerates the closed list (public, operational, directory, education_record, behavioral, sensitive, credential, privacy_governance, system) and `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` declares `INVALID_PRIVACY_CLASSES = {'depends_on_contents','depends_on_entity'}` with `validate_privacy_class()` failing the build on any depends_on_* placeholder or value outside the closed list.

`data/data-dictionary.seed.json` migration_generation block lists 13 relationship_keys, and `scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py` emits `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` from those `relationships[]` arrays with `--check` enforced by VERIFY.md. The validate_graph() function rejects relationships missing FK targets, cardinality, ownership, or delete_behavior.

Every projected field carries `decision_id` (e.g. `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json` person.email -> DEC-001). `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` enforces decision_id presence and validates the reference resolves in the decision-trace block of `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md`, failing on orphans.

Generators (`scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py`, `generate_qti_core.py`, etc.) emit Markdown and HTML dictionary pages from the per-spec JSON, and `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` validates that field descriptions, sourceStandard, and decision_id appear in every generated artifact. Hand-edited prose like `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` links each load-bearing claim to dictionary anchors (e.g. `oneroster-core-dictionary.html#person.email`).

The decision register itself is rendered on the portal and dictionary tables cite decision_ids, but there is no plain-language doc page that walks a reader through why each decision exists and what work it deletes — readers are sent to a 1700-line raw `standards-overlap-decisions.md` with terse 'Why not collapse' bullets. `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` and `docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md` reference decisions only via ID without prose explanation of the choice and its simplification.
items.traced_cause: docs/decisions-explained.md (or per-decision pages under docs/decisions/): write plain-language explanations linking each DEC-### choice to the artifact it simplified, with anchors back to the decision register.

items.reason: `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` covers all five required steps with copy-pasteable SQL and dictionary links, but step 4 instructs the reader to `create table public.build_app_assignment_case_links` — an app-owned table — admitting the platform has no CASE-linking surface, and step 5 inserts directly into `audit_log` and synthesizes a 'Caliper-style activity feed' via jsonb_build_object rather than reading the documented `caliper-event-ingestion` Edge Function. The guide also requires `SUPABASE_DB_URL` (a database password held in `.env.local`) which is never provisioned to readers.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md plus dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json and dictionary/case-core.v1.json: replace the app-owned alignment table and audit_log feed fabrication with a real CASE alignment endpoint and a Caliper feed reader sourced from the caliper-event-ingestion Edge Function, and document a credentialing path that doesn't depend on the database superuser URL.

items.reason: Generated dictionary HTML pages emit anchors (e.g. `#person.email`, `#enum.role_family.teacher`) and the build guide links to them, but there is no link-checker run committed (no report under `site/api/` or in VERIFY.md), so dead anchors cannot be verified. The build guide references `caliper-core-dictionary.html#enum.profile.GradingProfile` and `case-core-dictionary.html#enum.item_type.standard`, but the generator only emits anchors for enums actually used by fields — coverage of those specific values is not enforced by check_dictionary_artifacts.py.
items.traced_cause: VERIFY.md plus scripts/check_doc_links.py (new): add a link/anchor checker that walks every generated and hand-edited HTML page under site/ and fails on missing anchors, then commit its report under site/api/link-check.json.

items.reason: A layperson reading only `docs/` cannot complete the five-step scenario against the live runtime. The guide assumes `SUPABASE_DB_URL` is in the reader's shell, but `.env.example` ships it empty and no doc page tells the reader where to get it. Step 4 (link assignment to a CASE standard) is satisfied by creating an app-owned table — the platform exposes no CASE alignment endpoint — and step 5 (Caliper feed) is fabricated by inserting directly into `audit_log` and joining locally rather than calling the documented `caliper-event-ingestion` Edge Function. A layperson would correctly conclude these are not platform features.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md and dictionary/case-core.v1.json + dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json runtime projections: ship a real CASE alignment write/read surface (with dictionary entry, OpenAPI path, and Edge Function or RLS-backed table) and a real Caliper feed reader; document SUPABASE access credentials in docs/ rather than .env.local.

items.reason: `scripts/evaluate_platform.py` defines `DEFAULT_TRACED_CAUSES` per rubric id, `normalize_traced_cause()` enforces non-empty traced_cause for every non-pass verdict, and `normalize_buildability_gaps()` requires a traced_cause on each gap. The output schema documented in the script header includes traced_cause and buildability_gaps arrays.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, which the loop runs after every iteration via the fenced VERIFY block read by `scripts/codex_loop.py`. The evaluator writes a fresh report including traced_cause and buildability_gaps.

`scripts/codex_loop.py.read_overrides()` reads `docs/loop-overrides.md` at iteration start, `overrides_paused()` halts before the next iteration when a bare `PAUSE` token appears outside HTML comments, and `build_prompt()` injects the overrides under a 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE...)' section.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` terminates on `after_eval.get('done')` from the LLM evaluator both before iterating and after each iteration, with stall detection and max-iteration caps as safety rails. No numeric-threshold gate is used for termination.

buildability_gaps.gap: The guide depends on `SUPABASE_DB_URL` (a database superuser connection string) being preconfigured, but no doc page tells the reader how to obtain or set it; `.env.example` ships it empty and `docs/supabase-hosted-database.md` only references it for repo maintainers.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md and docs/supabase-hosted-database.md: document a layperson-accessible credential path (publishable key + JWT or scoped DB role), not the maintainer's SUPABASE_DB_URL.

buildability_gaps.gap: The guide instructs the reader to `create table public.build_app_assignment_case_links` themselves; the platform exposes no CASE alignment endpoint, dictionary write surface, or Edge Function for linking a line_item to a CASE item URI.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: dictionary/case-core.v1.json and supabase/migrations: add a runtime CASE-alignment table with dictionary entry, RLS policy, and either PostgREST or Edge Function write path so the developer doesn't have to invent app-owned schema.

buildability_gaps.gap: Step 5 inserts directly into `audit_log` and synthesizes a Caliper-shaped JSON envelope via jsonb_build_object joins, instead of reading from the documented `caliper-event-ingestion` Edge Function or a Caliper events table.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: supabase/functions/caliper-event-ingestion/index.ts and dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json: ship an immutable caliper_event table plus a tenant-scoped read endpoint, and rewrite the guide step to POST a GradeEvent to caliper-event-ingestion and GET the feed back.

buildability_gaps.gap: Steps 1-3 use direct psql INSERTs as the postgres superuser, bypassing the OAuth scopes, RLS, and audit policies that the rest of the docs declare are the platform's contract. A teaching-app developer following these instructions will not exercise any of the documented runtime gates.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: replace the psql block with a curl flow against PostgREST/Edge Functions using the publishable key and a JWT carrying tenant_id, so the steps go through the documented RLS and OAuth scope policies.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. QTI and CASE are doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated Edge Function receipt/governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, Caliper raw-event projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
